body:
#ad Creating a cozy outdoor living space is easier than it looks, especially when you start at @wayfair where there are so many great outdoor seating options. 

✨✨Comment”patio” for links✨✨

I like to begin with a rug to define the space, then add comfortable seating that makes people want to stay awhile. These chairs are especially cozy, and I added a couple of ottomans because they’re so versatile. They work perfectly as a footrest when you want to relax, or extra seating when you have people over.
The fire table really becomes the focal point of the whole setup. Once that’s in place, everything instantly feels warm, inviting, and like a space you actually want to spend time in outside.
Be sure to follow us on the @shop.LTK app INTERIORJEMS to see all our exclusive posts 🤍
https://liketk.it/5XrTn
#wishyouwerehere #outdoor #Wayfair WayfairPartner

body:
After finding so much of our furniture on Wayfair, I’m excited to say I’m officially living my @wayfair Creator era 🤍🛋️✨

I’ve gotten so many compliments from my story shares of our new home, so now they’re finally making their way onto my feed. :) And when it came to buying a couch, my husband and I definitely hit a few setbacks. Because of course…I had a whole wishlist. 😅

I wanted a modular (sectional pieces that can be rearranged anytime) in white or beige, washable, and with storage. Meanwhile, my husband’s only requirement? Affordable.

And guess what… he actually found the one on @wayfair. 👀

Not only is it modular and cozy with that soft corduroy texture, but it has storage in every single seat (yes, every one, not just the ottoman!). Adjustable armrests, plush cushions, neutral vibes… it instantly became the heart of our home.

Perfect for family movie nights, hosting friends, or hiding toys. 🤭

Feeling so excited to share this find with you as a #WayfairCreator and trust me, this won’t be the last piece I style from Wayfair. 🤍
.
.
.
.
Comment link to shop!

#Wayfair #livingroomfurniture #newhousedesign #homeorganizationtips #sectionalcouch

#ad 🗝 Our new favorite room (the patio) furnished with @Wayfair . Our black walnut tree fell during Hurricane Helene and for a year we had a pile of red dirt. Now we have a stone patio ready to host meals, stargazing and drinks by the firepit. 

My favorite part: the seating, surfaces and structures don’t need to be babied or protected every time a dark cloud crosses the sky. Every piece is hard working, easy to care for and timeless. I can spray the chairs and teak table off at the end of the season (wash the 2 little cushion slipcovers) and that’s it!

body:
🛋️ Couch Reset 💫

With two kids, this is exactly why washable couch cushions are a non-negotiable in our house. 😅🤍 So glad we took the risk buying this couch without trying it. Best decision we made in our new home. Thank you @wayfair !

Months later, here’s why we still love this sectional:
✔️ Washable covers = real mom life approved
✔️ Beige = still cute and timeless in our space
✔️ Modular = perfect for movie nights or used as trampoline 😅
✔️ Storage in every seat = less clutter, perfect to hide toys and seasonal pillow covers and blankets. 

Because homes are meant to be lived in, not just looked at. 🤍

Comment "shop" for direct link 🛍️

#Wayfair #WayfairCreator #cleanwithme #mommyslifestyle

I am loving our new beautiful kitchen island from @wayfair  It brings a clean, classic look with plenty of everyday function to our space. It has a sleek surface for meal prep, serving, or casual dining.

It has a single cabinet and four drawers to provide smart storage for cookware, utensils, or pantry items. The compact design makes it ideal for smaller kitchens.

I really love that I can easily change the direction from left to right or even to the center.  The direct link is in my bio today.

body:
Sometimes it’s not about adding more space… it’s about using the space you already have 🤍

Our small round breakfast table just wasn’t working anymore for our family of five, and the keeping room off our kitchen was barely being used. So I reimagined the layout and combined both areas into one large, functional breakfast space that finally fits our everyday life and when we’re entertaining.

Every piece in this makeover is from @wayfair — from the dining table and chairs to the rug, black sideboards, and the brushed gold chandelier that completely transformed the space ✨ The difference in how this room functions is everything.

If you’re feeling stuck with a space in your home, let this be your sign to rethink the layout.

✨ Save this for layout inspiration
✨ Comment SHOP if you want the links to shop this room, or find everything linked in my Wayfair storefront (link in bio)
✨ Follow along for more home projects & affordable finds

The playroom era is officially evolving 🥹✨ Comment SHOP for a link 🔗 

We just added this small sectional + rug to start transitioning the girls’ space from toy chaos to “big girl” hangout + study zone… and I’m honestly not ready for how fast this shift feels.

I wanted something cozy enough for movie nights and sleepovers, but structured enough to grow with them over the next few years — and this combo from @wayfair was exactly what I had in mind. Soft, neutral, practical, and still pretty.

If you’re in that in-between stage of playroom → tween retreat, I linked everything in my Wayfair storefront so you can see the exact sectional + rug (and a few other pieces I’m eyeing for the space).

body:
I used to save the “pretty” things. The good candles. The nice dishes. The special linens. I’d tell myself I was waiting for the right moment.

But here’s the truth: this is the moment.

You don’t create a beautiful home just to look at it. You create it to live in it. To use it. To enjoy it on an ordinary Tuesday.

Light the candle.
Use the good plates.
Sit in the room you worked so hard to design.

A cozy, intentional home isn’t about perfection — it’s about participation. It’s about letting your space support your everyday life, not just your guests.

Live in your house.
Use the pretty stuff. #wayfairelevate #wayfaircreator #ad @wayfair #CozyHome #charlottedesigner

body:
Sometimes it’s not about adding more space… it’s about using the space you already have 🤍

Our small round breakfast table just wasn’t working anymore for our family of five, and the keeping room off our kitchen was barely being used. So I reimagined the layout and combined both areas into one large, functional breakfast space that finally fits our everyday life and when we’re entertaining.

Every piece in this makeover is from @wayfair — from the dining table and chairs to the rug, black sideboards, and the brushed gold chandelier that completely transformed the space ✨ The difference in how this room functions is everything.

If you’re feeling stuck with a space in your home, let this be your sign to rethink the layout.

✨ Save this for layout inspiration
✨ Comment SHOP if you want the links to shop this room, or find everything linked in my Wayfair storefront (link in bio)
✨ Follow along for more home projects & affordable finds

body:
I didn’t realize pillows actually need to be replaced regularly… until I learned how much they impact sleep quality.

Over time pillows lose support and collect allergens, which can quietly affect rest, comfort, and neck alignment.

As part of a bedroom refresh, I upgraded to the Lauren by Ralph Lauren Luxury Cotton Pillow Two-Pack from Wayfair, designed for all sleep positions and that soft, hotel-bed feel at home.

Sometimes the smallest changes make the biggest difference in how well we rest 🤍

Save this for when it’s time to replace your pillows

@wayfair has been my go-to for area rugs for our new house! We have hardwood floors throughout, so I’ve been adding area rugs to:

🩷Protect our original hardwood floors
💚Provide warmth and cushioning under foot
💛Define seating areas and anchor our furniture
💜Provide sound absorption
🩵Add color, pattern + personality to each space

#Wayfair has THOUSANDS of area rugs to choose from, so I like to narrow my search by size, color, and price maxing out at $500. And still I had hundreds to choose from! Whether you’re furnishing a new home like us or looking to refresh an existing space, area rugs are like art for your floors, offering style and functionality in one.

Comment RUG and I’ll send you the link to my Wayfair storefront to shop my favorite area rugs! #ad #WayfairElevate #WayfairCreator

body:
The playroom era is officially evolving 🥹✨ Comment SHOP for a link 🔗 

We just added this small sectional + rug to start transitioning the girls’ space from toy chaos to “big girl” hangout + study zone… and I’m honestly not ready for how fast this shift feels.

I wanted something cozy enough for movie nights and sleepovers, but structured enough to grow with them over the next few years — and this combo from @wayfair was exactly what I had in mind. Soft, neutral, practical, and still pretty.

If you’re in that in-between stage of playroom → tween retreat, I linked everything in my Wayfair storefront so you can see the exact sectional + rug (and a few other pieces I’m eyeing for the space). 

Shop here: https://creatorsold.io/wayfair/lauren_loves_gifting

All of these rugs are vintage inspired and I rarely have this good of luck finding rugs for my home!  I would be happy with any of these!  And they could easily be in a family room or bedroom!  These runners are ALL under $100!

body:
Remember the reel where I showed how I tested this light fixture in a photo before committing to buy it?

This is the reveal.

One of the reasons I always visualize design choices first is because lighting changes the entire feeling of a space — scale, balance, and the overall atmosphere of the room.

Seeing it in the design plan beforehand gave me the confidence to move forward with the purchase, and now you can see the finished result installed in the space.

This is the same process I use in my design consultations and what I teach homeowners inside my course — how to visualize a room before committing to furniture, lighting, or decor.

If you’re in Charlotte or Waxhaw, I offer in-person design consultations, and I also work virtually with homeowners across the U.S.

Follow along if you want to learn more about designing with confidence before you buy. #wayfair #wayfairelevate #wayfaircreator #ad #staircaselight

Sharing some of my new home finds that I recently ordered from @wayfair All of these pieces are budget-friendly and perfect for a neutral, timeless home.

I also swapped out my heavy glass chandelier for this light-weighted oversized one, and I’m loving the overall vibe it brings to the space.

The mirror is a little on the pricier side, but the design is classic, has been around for years, and will never go out of style. The quality feels very high-end and absolutely worth the investment.

Comment **APP** and I’ll send you the link 🤍

While we were building our home, I kept a running list in the Wayfair app called *“New Build 2024.”* Anytime I found something I loved, I saved it there so I could come back to it later when we were ready to make decisions.

So many pieces in our home now actually started on that list — from our kitchen barn door to the pocket door locks, my son’s bathroom mirror, our freestanding tub, and our island lights.

One thing I’ve always relied on when choosing pieces is the reviews. Seeing real customer feedback has helped guide so many of my decisions, and it’s never let me down.

Follow for more Wayfair finds and home inspiration from thetidytrendy ✨

These 3 pieces completely changed the feel of our outdoor spaces and made everything look more styled and intentional:

• Front porch planters that frame the entry and make the house feel welcoming before anyone even walks inside

• A small bistro set for quiet morning coffee or a place for guests to sit and chat

I love mixing pieces like these because they make the outside of our home feel just as designed and comfortable as the inside.

All of these Wayfair finds are linked in my bio and @shop.ltk so you can shop them easily. https://liketk.it/5ZntJ

body:
Remember the reel where I showed how I tested this light fixture in a photo before committing to buy it?

This is the reveal.

One of the reasons I always visualize design choices first is because lighting changes the entire feeling of a space — scale, balance, and the overall atmosphere of the room.

Seeing it in the design plan beforehand gave me the confidence to move forward with the purchase, and now you can see the finished result installed in the space.

This is the same process I use in my design consultations and what I teach homeowners inside my course — how to visualize a room before committing to furniture, lighting, or decor.

If you’re in Charlotte or Waxhaw, I offer in-person design consultations, and I also work virtually with homeowners across the U.S.

Follow along if you want to learn more about designing with confidence before you buy. #wayfair #wayfairelevate #wayfaircreator #ad #staircaselight

Sharing some of my new home finds that I recently ordered from @wayfair All of these pieces are budget-friendly and perfect for a neutral, timeless home.

The coffee table came fully assembled, and I love the hidden drawers for extra storage. I’m obsessed with my new sofa; the seating is deep and comfortable, and all the covers are removable, which makes washing so easy. I also swapped out my heavy glass chandelier for this light-weighted oversized one, and I’m loving the overall vibe it brings to the space.

The mirror is a little on the pricier side, but the design is classic, has been around for years, and will never go out of style. The quality feels very high-end and absolutely worth the investment.
